prompt: show me what an invoicing dashboard for my freelance design business would look like - list of invoices with statuses, create a new invoice, mark one paid, and some kind of monthly revenue chart. make it something i can actually click through, not just a picture
assertions: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
The invoice list shows a status per invoice (e.g. draft/sent/paid/overdue)
The create-invoice flow adds a new invoice to the visible list in memory
Marking an invoice paid changes its status and the change is reflected in the UI (totals and/or chart update)
A monthly revenue chart renders from the hardcoded data
An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
